data.title: [Agent Routing]: dso:completion-verifier subagent_type dispatch fails despite plugin being installed
data.description:
## Expected Behavior

When the sprint orchestrator dispatches `dso:completion-verifier` via the Agent tool with `subagent_type='dso:completion-verifier'`, the Agent tool should route to the agent definition at `plugins/dso/agents/completion-verifier.md` (or the equivalent installed cache at `~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/digital-service-orchestra/agents/completion-verifier.md`).

All 12+ named dso agents are defined in `plugins/dso/agents/`:
- code-reviewer-light.md, code-reviewer-standard.md, code-reviewer-deep-*.md
- completion-verifier.md
- bot-psychologist.md
- etc.

## Actual Behavior

In session worktree-20260409-084011 (and in multiple prior sessions per memory entries debug_session_20260408c.md and debug_session_20260409.md), when the orchestrator attempts to use named agents like `dso:code-reviewer-light`, `dso:code-reviewer-standard`, or `dso:completion-verifier` via the Agent tool's subagent_type parameter, the dispatch fails with an error like 'Unknown skill' or the agent is silently unavailable.

The session transcript shows the repeated workaround: the orchestrator reads the agent .md file directly (e.g., `cat plugins/dso/agents/completion-verifier.md`) and dispatches it as a general-purpose agent with the content inlined in the prompt. This produces functionally correct behavior but:

1. Violates the agent dispatch contract (CLAUDE.md table of named agents)
2. Adds latency (extra Read tool call + larger prompt)
3. Loses any model-tier enforcement (e.g., completion-verifier is model:sonnet; inline dispatch uses whatever the orchestrator's model is)
4. Obscures failures — if the dispatch silently falls back instead of failing hard, the orchestrator has no way to detect the routing is broken

## Evidence From This Session

The session summary explicitly notes: 'Named review agents unavailable: dso:code-reviewer-* not available via Agent tool. Fix: read agent definition .md files and dispatch as general-purpose agents with inline content.'

CLAUDE.md states: 'Agent fallback: On dispatch failure, read plugins/dso/agents/<agent-name>.md inline (strip dso: prefix from subagent_type). Tests: tests/hooks/test-sub-agent-guard.sh.'

The fallback exists for technical failures (timeout, crash), but is being triggered on EVERY dispatch attempt, suggesting this is a persistent installation or routing problem — not an isolated failure.

## Agent Definitions Affected

All agents in plugins/dso/agents/ with dso: prefix subagent_type:
- dso:completion-verifier (model: sonnet) — verifies SC/DD per sprint Phase 7 Step 0.75
- dso:code-reviewer-light (model: haiku) — tier 0-2 reviews
- dso:code-reviewer-standard (model: sonnet) — tier 3-6 reviews  
- dso:code-reviewer-deep-* (model: sonnet + opus) — tier 7+ reviews
- dso:bot-psychologist (model: sonnet) — LLM behavioral fix
- dso:red-test-writer (model: sonnet) — RED test generation
- dso:intent-search (model: sonnet) — Gate 1a in fix-bug

## Suspected Root Cause

The DSO plugin installs agents to `~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/digital-service-orchestra/agents/`. The Agent tool's subagent_type routing likely looks for agents in a specific registry path or manifest. If the plugin's agent manifest is not correctly structured, stale, or the Agent tool version changed its routing lookup format, all dso: prefixed agent dispatches fail silently.

Worktree isolation may also contribute: worktree sessions at `/Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra-worktrees/worktree-20260409-084011/` may not have the same plugin cache path resolved as the main repo at `/Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra/`.

## Impact

Critical: every sprint uses named agents for review (code-reviewer-*) and verification (completion-verifier). If all named agent dispatches silently fall back to inline content, the model tier enforcement (haiku/sonnet/opus selection) is bypassed for every review and verification call. The orchestrator may use the wrong model or fail to detect dispatch errors.

## Fix Directions

1. Verify that `~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/digital-service-orchestra/agents/` contains the agent .md files and that the plugin.json manifest lists them
2. Check if the Agent tool's subagent_type parameter is case-sensitive and whether `dso:completion-verifier` vs `completion-verifier` routing differs
3. Test `subagent_type='dso:completion-verifier'` from a non-worktree session vs a worktree session to isolate the environment dependency
4. Add a startup check in /dso:sprint that verifies named agent routing works before the first dispatch attempt
5. Consider making the inline fallback emit a WARNING to stderr so the orchestrator knows routing is degraded
